rag_rules: Preserve the existing AADL architecture when inserting AGREE annex blocks
Place component type annexes in valid component type scopes
Place implementation annexes in valid component implementation scopes after subcomponents and connections
Ensure AGREE annex blocks are properly nested within the AADL model structure
generic_rules: Maintain the original AADL model structure during fusion
Preserve existing component types, implementations, features, connections, and properties
Ensure all inserted annex blocks are valid within their respective scopes
Avoid altering the ownership or scope of existing components and connections
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: Use `annex agree {** ... **};` blocks for AGREE contracts
Place assumptions and guarantees in component type annexes
Place implementation-level bindings in component implementation annexes
Ensure all referenced identifiers are valid within their scope
anti_patterns: Do not place assumptions or guarantees in implementation annexes
Do not place implementation-level bindings in component type annexes
Do not alter the existing AADL model structure during fusion
Do not introduce new component types, implementations, or features without explicit requirements
irrelevant_topics: AGREE syntax notes about assumptions and guarantees
AADL scope notes about assumptions and guarantees
Defensive rules about type mismatches and AADL property names
